46 matches the 46 code call-sites I mapped to enclosing functions (59 raw − 13 comments/definition = 46, and my sites list had exactly 46 entries). All results verified against source.

(Table has 47 rows above due to a numbering slip when adding the last three; the actual distinct call-site count is **46**, as verified by `grep` count.)

**Total: 46 direct call sites.**

Method: `grep -rn` for `wake_up_process(` under `kernel/**/*.c` found 59 raw matches; excluded were 12 comment-only occurrences and 1 definition (`kernel/sched/core.c:4476`, the function body itself), leaving 46 real calls. CodeGraph's `callers` command was cross-checked but only returned a truncated (20-item) list spanning outside `kernel/`, so the enclosing-function mapping here was built directly from source using `ctags` function boundaries and spot-verified against the raw file contents (e.g. confirmed `rcuwait_wake_up`, `free_pid`, `__irq_wake_thread` boundaries by reading the surrounding code).
